MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-13-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS STAFFING GROUP, LLC, EMPLOYER'S OUTSOURCING, INC., AND KALIFORNIA ADMINISTRATORS, INC.'S DEMURRER: RESPONDENTS' DEMURRER TO THE "FIRST AMENDED PETITION AND NOTICE OF ELECTION TO WITHDRAW FROM ARBITRATION PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1281.98" IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE ONLY PETITION AUTHORIZED BY CCP 1281.98 IS ONE TO COMPEL PAYMENT OF ARBITRATION FEES. (ID. AT (B)(3).) NO PETITION "TO WITHDRAW FROM ARBITRATION" IS MENTIONED IN THE STATUTE, THOUGH THE LEGISLATURE SHOWED IN (B)(3) THAT IT KNEW HOW TO AUTHORIZE A PETITION WHEN IT SO INTENDED. CCP 1281.98(B) AUTHORIZES SEVERAL COURSES OF ACTION. GIVEN THE ABOVE RULING, JURISDICTIONAL ISSUES NEED NOT BE REACHED. ONE ORDER SIGNED FOR BOTH DEMURRERS. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On First Amended Petition And Notice Of Election To Withdraw From Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 1281.98
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Anthony Lewis (alewis@lewisla.com) for Petitioners Janet Anunda, et al.
Kieran Ringgenberg (kieran@ringgenberglaw.com) for Respondents, MONTEREY TILTH LLC, et al.
Ron Gomez (rgomez@koreyrichardsonlaw.com) for Respondents, Personnel Staffing Group, LLC, et al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JANET ANUNDA'S First Amended Petition And Notice Of Election To Withdraw From Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 1281.98 is Off calendar per the rulings on the demurrers.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Anthony Lewis (alewis@lewisla.com) for Petitioners Janet Anunda, et al.
Kieran Ringgenberg (kieran@ringgenberglaw.com) for Respondents, MONTEREY TILTH LLC, et al.
Ron Gomez (rgomez@koreyrichardsonlaw.com) for Respondents, Personnel Staffing Group, LLC, et al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTs CHRISTINA DIPACI, ELOE MARAVILLA, and MONTEREY TILTH LLC's DEMURRER:

Respondents' demurrer to the "first amended petition and notice of election to withdraw from arbitration pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 1281.98" is sustained without leave to amend. The only petition authorized by CCP 1281.98 is one to compel payment of arbitration fees. (Id. at (b)(3).) No petition "to withdraw from arbitration" is mentioned in the statute, though the Legislature showed in (b)(3) that it knew how to authorize a petition when it so intended. CCP 1281.98(b) authorizes several courses of action. Given the above ruling, jurisdictional issues need not be reached.

one order signed for both demurrers.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Anthony Lewis (alewis@lewisla.com) for Petitioners Janet Anunda, et al.
Kieran Ringgenberg (kieran@ringgenberglaw.com) for Respondents, MONTEREY TILTH LLC, et al.
Ron Gomez (rgomez@koreyrichardsonlaw.com) for Respondents, Personnel Staffing Group, LLC, et al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTS STAFFING GROUP, LLC, EMPLOYER'S OUTSOURCING, INC., and KALIFORNIA ADMINISTRATORS, INC.'S DEMURRER

Respondents' demurrer to the "first amended petition and notice of election to withdraw from arbitration pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 1281.98" is sustained without leave to amend. The only petition authorized by CCP 1281.98 is one to compel payment of arbitration fees. (Id. at (b)(3).) No petition "to withdraw from arbitration" is mentioned in the statute, though the Legislature showed in (b)(3) that it knew how to authorize a petition when it so intended. CCP 1281.98(b) authorizes several courses of action. Given the above ruling, jurisdictional issues need not be reached.

one order signed for both demurrers.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
